what_is_implemented: The branch tree includes live Euria runtime assessment in gateway/app.py and gateway tests.
The current merge delta adds PB-010 through PB-014 governance control documentation, scripts, tests, and extensive evidence artifacts while modifying PB-006 through PB-009 evidence inputs.
what_is_documentation_only: PB-010 through PB-014 governance docs under docs/governance.
Some pilot and architecture Euria files in the branch tree are documentation-only and not part of the current merge delta.
what_is_runtime_functionality: Euria live assessment endpoint and control-plane rendering exist in the target branch tree.
PB governance utilities are executable local validation scripts, not production service activation.
what_advances_usbay_automation: Automates local governance certification, drift detection, control registry validation, monitoring, and recovery validation.
Strengthens evidence-chain automation needed before production connector activation.
what_should_be_merged_first: Do not merge the whole branch as-is until full-suite validation and artifact-size/recovery-workspace review are resolved.
Consider splitting Euria runtime endpoint/control-plane changes from PB-005 through PB-014 evidence automation if those changes are not already present in main.

Merge smaller reviewed PB control slices in dependency order: PB-006 integrity, PB-007 independent verification, PB-008 timestamp, PB-009 archive, PB-010 chain certification, PB-011 drift, PB-012 registry, PB-013 monitor, PB-014 recovery.
what_should_be_deferred: Large generated backup/recovery workspaces until artifact retention policy is reviewed
Any production-readiness or external certification claim
dependencies: PB-005 evidence artifacts feed PB-006 through PB-014 controls.
PB-006 integrity precedes PB-007 independent verification.
PB-008 timestamp depends on signed/integrity manifests.
PB-009 archive depends on PB-005 through PB-008 artifacts.
PB-010 certification depends on PB-005 through PB-009 reports.
PB-011 drift depends on PB-010 baseline.
PB-012 registry depends on PB-005 through PB-011 control definitions.
PB-013 monitor depends on PB-012 registry plus PB-010/PB-011 reports.
PB-014 recovery depends on PB-010/PB-011/PB-012/PB-013 outputs.

final_answers.what_is_implemented: Live Euria runtime assessment exists in the target branch tree; current merge delta primarily implements PB-010 through PB-014 governance evidence automation and carries PB-006 through PB-009 updates.
final_answers.what_is_documentation_only: PB governance docs and Euria architecture/pilot materials are documentation-only.
final_answers.what_is_runtime_functionality: Gateway Euria assessment endpoint, control-plane rendering, and PB local governance validation scripts are executable runtime/local automation surfaces.
final_answers.what_advances_usbay_automation: Local governance chain certification, drift detection, registry validation, continuous monitoring, recovery validation, and fail-closed Euria assessment tests.
final_answers.what_should_be_merged_first: Merge only after validation blockers resolve; if split, merge PB controls in dependency order and keep Euria runtime changes separately if not already in main.
final_answers.what_should_be_deferred: Production activation, live Euria calls, credentials, external synchronization, live deployments, and large recovery workspace retention until reviewed.
final_answers.what_dependencies_exist: PB-006 through PB-014 form a chained dependency graph over PB-005 evidence artifacts; Euria runtime depends on gateway fail-closed policy and human approval semantics.
